title: type keyword is overloaded for struct definitions, type aliases, and constrained types
description: Ferrum uses 'type Point { x: i32, y: i32 }' for struct definitions, 'type Meters = f32' for aliases, and 'type Percent = u8 where value <= 100' for constrained types. All three use the same keyword. A new reader cannot tell from 'type X { ... }' whether X is a struct or something else — they must read the body. The word 'type' is already overloaded in type system discourse (type parameters, type inference, type system). Using it for struct definitions specifically loses the product-type shape signal that 'struct' provides. Consider: 'struct' for product types, 'type' only for aliases and constraints, or at minimum add clear visual distinction in the syntax.
close_reason: Added product types to §1.4 with design decision note: intended direction is struct for product types, type for aliases/constraints. Spec examples not yet updated — mechanical rename tracked as follow-on.
